title: Cyanotis cristata
intro: Cyanotis cristata, also known as the blue star or Indian blue star, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Commelinaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyanotis cristata include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyanotis cristata is commonly prepared as an infusion or a decoction, as well as being used in the form of a powder or a syrup.
taxonomy: Cyanotis cristata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to India and Sri Lanka. The Commelinaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the dayflowers or spiderworts. Cyanotis cristata is also sometimes referred to as the blue dayflower or blue spiderwort. This plant is a herbaceous perennial, meaning it dies back to the ground after flowering.
